clrs.text: What does O(n) notation mean precisely? Give the formal definition involving constants c and n₀.
What is the difference between O, Ω, Θ, o, and ω? When does Θ(n) not exist for an algorithm?

What is the difference between worst-case, average-case, and best-case complexity? Which does O-notation describe?
Rank the following from slowest to fastest growth: n!, 2ⁿ, nⁿ, n³, n² log n, n log n, n, log n, log log n, 1.
What is amortized complexity? How is it different from average-case complexity?
Prove that log(n!) = Θ(n log n) using Stirling's approximation.

What is the difference between an algorithm's time complexity and its practical running time?
Show that if f(n) = O(g(n)) and g(n) = O(h(n)), then f(n) = O(h(n)). (Transitivity of O.)
What is the RAM model of computation? What operations cost O(1) and what are its limitations?
Prove that max(f(n), g(n)) = Θ(f(n) + g(n)).

What is recursion depth and how does it affect space complexity?
